name: Known Process Started with a Different Hash
notes: This rule triggers when a known process is started with an unknown hash.
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event QID is one of the following (5001828) Process Create
when any of Process Name (custom) are contained in any of ProcessNametoHashRefMapOfSetKeys - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
when any of Process Name (custom) is the key and any of File Hash (custom) is the value in any of ProcessNametoHash - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
TD_list: APPLY Known Process Started with a Different Hash on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event QID is one of the following (5001828) Process Create
AND when any of Process Name (custom) are contained in any of ProcessNametoHashRefMapOfSetKeys - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
AND NOT when any of Process Name (custom) is the key and any of File Hash (custom) is the value in any of ProcessNametoHash - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
RED_list: Event name: Detected a Known Process Started with a New Unseen Hash
Event description: Detects if a known process will be started with a new hash, it can help to detect some privilege escalation techniques
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
